Class CreateQrFragment

java.lang.Object
androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
com.example.codebase.CreateQrFragment
All Implemented Interfaces:
androidx.activity.result.ActivityResultCaller, androidx.lifecycle.HasDefaultViewModelProviderFactory, androidx.lifecycle.LifecycleOwner, androidx.lifecycle.ViewModelStoreOwner, androidx.savedstate.SavedStateRegistryOwner
public class CreateQrFragment extends androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
Fragment for the final step ("Created") of the create-event wizard.

Displays the event name and the QR code

invalid reference 
android.graphics.Bitmap
generated by CreateEventActivity#generateQr(String) after a successful Firestore write. The organiser can also share the QR code image to other apps via the system share sheet.

UI population is deferred to onResume() rather than onCreateView(LayoutInflater, ViewGroup, Bundle) because CreateEventViewModel.generatedQr is set asynchronously after the Firestore operation completes and the ViewPager advances to this step; by the time onResume() runs the bitmap is guaranteed to be available.

See Also:
  • CreateEventActivity
  • CreateEventViewModel

  • Method Details

    • onCreateView

      @Nullable public View onCreateView(@NonNull LayoutInflater inflater, @Nullable ViewGroup container, @Nullable Bundle savedInstanceState)
      Inflates the fragment layout and obtains the shared CreateEventViewModel. No view binding or data population is performed here; see onResume().
      Overrides:
      onCreateView in class androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
      Parameters:
      inflater - The
      invalid reference 
      LayoutInflater
      used to inflate the view.
      container - The parent
      invalid reference 
      ViewGroup
      , or null.
      savedInstanceState - Previously saved state, or null.
      Returns:
      The inflated root
      invalid reference 
      View
      for this fragment.

    • onResume

      public void onResume()
      Populates the UI with the event name and QR code once the fragment is visible.

      Binds the following views when both Fragment.getView() and CreateEventViewModel.generatedQr are non-null:

      • tvEventNameDisplay – set to CreateEventViewModel.name.
      • ivQrCode – set to the
        invalid reference 
        android.graphics.Bitmap
        in CreateEventViewModel.generatedQr.
      • btnShare – wired to shareQrCode().

      If either the view or the QR bitmap is not yet available this method is a no-op, avoiding a NullPointerException if the fragment becomes visible before the async Firestore write has completed.

      Overrides:
      onResume in class androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
